bullets.text: Full-year 2013 combined ratio was 93.5, producing pretax underwriting income of $1.1 billion; comprehensive income per share of $2.06 was up 16% with ROE of ~19%, both markedly better than 2012.

Written premiums grew ~6% to top $17 billion for the year; early-year growth was rate-driven, but the final third saw a return to unit-driven growth as new application rates turned double-digit in both channels.

Net 276,000 auto customers were added for the year, with most gains in the last four months; both Agency and Direct channels reported double-digit new application growth by mid-Q3, reversing the rate-increase-driven decline.

Snapshot contributed over $2 billion to auto written premium; mobile transactions approached 25% of all quoting and sales; Progressive was recognized as No. 1 insurance website by Keynote for the ninth consecutive year.

Variable dividend of ~$0.49/share plus $1.00 special dividend were declared for 2013; equity portfolio returned 32.8% and fixed-income returned 1.7%; pretax book yield was 2.6% with short-duration positioning maintained.
Agency channel ended the year with a 2% reduction in auto policyholders despite modest premium growth; Direct channel sold over 2 million policies, setting a new high-watermark for sales in a year.
Flo's Superstore campaign performed at its best response levels yet; 'Rate Suckers' and 'Policy Box' campaigns expanded Snapshot messaging across a wider consumer spectrum.

Progressive Home Advantage served over 1 million customers and was available in 23 states; nine new Service Centers opened in 2013 bringing the total to 63, with Service Centers representing over a third of physical damage claims in served areas.

Claims organization achieved strong results on quality, efficiency, and customer experience metrics despite multiple catastrophe events including fires, tornadoes, flooding, and hail throughout the year.

Annual letter honored founder Peter B. Lewis who passed away late in the year; reprinted the Progressive Business Model (96 combined ratio as constant, grow as fast as possible) and Peter Lewis's final CEO letter from 2000.
